title: delissea hirtella var. subglabra
intro: Delissea hirtella var. subglabra, also known as the Hawaiian mountain bellflower, is a type of flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. The medicinal properties of Delissea hirtella var. subglabra are primarily due to its bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe and protect the body from infections and diseases. Delissea hirtella var. subglabra is traditionally prepared as an herbal tea or infused in hot water to release its medicinal properties, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
